principles: No ICT, SMC, order blocks, chart patterns or discretionary labels.
No parameter is selected on the final evaluation segment.
Signals are formed only from information timestamped as available before execution.
Publication lags are explicit for COT, macro releases and financial reports.
All costs are charged on actual turnover; futures include roll costs and options include spread and delta-hedge costs.
Instrument trades are not treated as independent statistical observations.
A failed final segment is consumed and cannot be recycled as a fresh holdout.

hypotheses.economic_rationale: Slow-moving hedging demand, behavioural under-reaction and institutional position adjustment create persistent trends across unrelated futures markets.
hypotheses.universe: Liquid equity-index, rate, FX, energy, metal and agricultural futures discovered from the LSE catalog.
hypotheses.signal: Equal-weighted signs of 1, 3, 6 and 12 month excess returns, scaled by 36 month realised volatility.

hypotheses.economic_rationale: Roll yield compensates inventory, convenience-yield and financing imbalances and is distinct from outright direction.
hypotheses.universe: Futures roots with at least two simultaneously traded contracts and reliable expiry metadata.
hypotheses.signal: Annualised front-to-next log-price slope, winsorised cross-sectionally within asset class; hold until the earlier of monthly rebalance or roll window.

hypotheses.economic_rationale: Commercial hedgers and leveraged funds carry different information and risk premia; extreme positioning can forecast medium-horizon returns.
hypotheses.universe: Futures roots with COT history and tradeable price history.
hypotheses.signal: Commercial net-position percentile minus managed-money percentile over a fixed 156-week window.
hypotheses.execution_lag: Next Monday close after the CFTC publication timestamp

hypotheses.economic_rationale: Currency risk premia are related to interest-rate differentials, external balances and long-run valuation, but crash risk must be explicitly controlled.
hypotheses.universe: G10 USD crosses with matching policy-rate or two-year-yield series.
hypotheses.signal: 50% lagged yield differential and 50% five-year real-exchange-rate deviation; volatility and global-risk scaling fixed ex ante.
hypotheses.execution_lag: One full session after all macro inputs are available

hypotheses.economic_rationale: Index option sellers are compensated for warehousing crash and convexity risk; the premium should be measured against subsequently realised variance, not option direction.
hypotheses.universe: SPX/SPY and other index underlyings with historical option prints, IV, greeks and contract candles.
hypotheses.signal: Sell delta-hedged 30-to-45 DTE variance only when model-free implied variance exceeds trailing 21-day realised variance by a fixed volatility-scaled threshold.
hypotheses.execution_lag: Next tradable quote after signal timestamp

hypotheses.economic_rationale: Index correlation risk is priced differently from single-name variance, creating a diversified relative-value premium.
hypotheses.universe: Index and liquid constituents with simultaneous option history.
hypotheses.signal: Long constituent variance and short index variance at matched vega when implied correlation exceeds its expanding historical median.

hypotheses.economic_rationale: The market response to standardised macro surprises differs across rates, FX and equity futures and can be traded cross-sectionally after release.
hypotheses.universe: US, euro-area and UK CPI, labour, growth and central-bank events with forecast and actual values plus intraday futures data.
hypotheses.signal: Expanding-window standardised actual-minus-consensus surprise mapped to a predeclared cross-asset response matrix.
hypotheses.execution_lag: Thirty minutes after the recorded release time
